cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The problem is about figuring out the correct date one year ago from today, given that John is correct about today's date. Jane thinks today is 6/18/2019, but John says it's 6/19/2019, and he's right. So today is June 19, 2019. The question asks for the date one year ago from today, in MM/DD/YYYY format. The options are A through F. Let me think through this step by step. First, if today is June 19, 2019, then one year ago would be June 19, 2018. But wait, I need to check if there's any catch here. Sometimes when dealing with dates, especially around leap years or month ends, there can be nuances. For example, if today was March 1, 2020, and you go back one year, it would be March 1, 2019. But if today was February 29, 2020 (a leap year), then one year ago would be February 28, 2019, because 2019 is not a leap year. However, in this case, June 19 is a date that exists in both 2019 and 2018. 2019 is not a leap year, but that doesn't affect June. So going back exactly one year would just subtract 1 from the year, keeping the month and day the same. So June 19, 2019 minus one year is June 19, 2018. Looking at the options, option A is 06/19/2018. Wait, option A is labeled as (A) 06/19/2018. But looking at the options given, let me check again. The options are: (A) 06/19/2018 (B) 05/22/2018 (C) 06/19/1934 (D) 06/19/1956
